item.description:
Morning Log

I sleep|slept decent, woke up to Maza attacking my feet around 6, watched the clouds and smoked phezi until around 8, when I let myself into Terran’s room and napped until maybe 9. Got up and made coffee and a couple [fried eggs](fried eggs) with turmeric and [black pepper](black pepper) for myself and Terran.

plan|Planning to use the day to sit on the Lanai with [Beading Projects|my beading](Beading Projects|my beading) and [my personal computer](my personal computer) and just kinda… bask in a stable life and see what work looks like - but there’s a couple chore|chores to get to: dishwashing|dishes have to come first, and I’d like to [domestic logistics|sort the canned goods in the kitchen](domestic logistics|sort the canned goods in the kitchen). Another thing I want to get to is another round of [my exercise routine|working out](my exercise routine|working out); I may have shared (or not) that I was working to create a personal routine based off drawing from a [deck of playing cards](deck of playing cards). And, part of the computer use today is I want to start/resume keeping [digital record|digital records](digital record|digital records), as evidenced by this log entry. I think in the recent past I was overcomplicating the taxonomy of files and headlines in a way that was unproductive, so my current stance is to have a collection of operational files (like this log, or one for tasks like dishes and cabinet sorting), an encyclopedia of specific terms, people, places, times, etc., and then simply a flat directory of documents. An unstated but important part of this is that I am using ChatGPT, but not directly in my editor: I’m currently using the Web interface, and copy-pasting (or re-transcribing) relevant information between the two.

item.title: Business Continuity & Disaster recovery
item.description:
Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery Architecture

Describes strategies for cross-region replication, failover, and recovery from catastrophic failures.

Dependencies

  • Storage/Replication specs: provide snapshot and replication semantics.
  • Scheduler spec: orchestrates cross-region cutovers and periodic tests.
  • Audit spec: logs recovery actions.
  • Policy spec: enforces authorization and separation-of-duty controls.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: standardizes telemetry (RPO/RTO, lag) during drills and incidents.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures region/topology placements match earned coverage constraints during failover.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: ties every runbook step back to the acts and specs that justify it.

Components

  1. Multi-Region Replication.
    Extends replication controller to multiple regions; ensures eventual consistency.

item.title: Data Catalog & Metadata Service
item.description:
Data Catalog & Metadata Service Architecture

Tracks all stored recognitions, their contexts, lineage, and usage.

Dependencies

  • Storage spec: snapshot definitions.
  • Audit spec: provenance trails.
  • Introspection spec: captures usage metrics.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: feeds read/write telemetry and usage statistics into the catalog for analysis.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: lets the catalog embed references so every dataset/version can be traced back to the acts/specs that produced it.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: provides placement/coverage data so catalog entries know which strata/regions contain a dataset.

Components

  1. Catalog DB.
    Stores metadata about snapshots, contexts, modules that produced them, policy classifications, and topology placement (region/stratum) details.

item.title: Deployment & upgrade Flow
item.description:
Deployment & Upgrade Flow Architecture

Defines how modules are deployed, upgraded, and rolled back while preserving relational invariants.

Dependencies

  • Storage/Replication specs: snapshot/replica management for stateful modules.
  • Testing spec: run automated tests before rollout.
  • Policy spec: restrict who can deploy/rollback.
  • Scheduler spec: coordinate upgrade tasks (drain traffic, restart modules).
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: supplies SLO/SLA telemetry to verify upgrade health.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: proves each deployment step references the earned acts/specs that justify it.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures rollouts respect placement/order constraints derived from towers/sheaves.

Phases

  1. Preparation.

item.title: Directed Space compiler
item.description:
Directed Space Compiler Architecture

Generates the directed spaces, flows, and stratified sites consumed by runtime, analytics, and reasoning modules.

Dependencies

  • Directed Homotopy Simulation: supplies flow/nucleus mechanics and tower coherence checks for oriented paths.
  • Directed Stratified Site Simulation: provides strata definitions, coverage, and tower templates for each module family.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: consumes compiled artifacts; this service validates that emitted bundles satisfy the integration rules.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: exports compiler diagnostics (coherence checks, coverage errors) for monitoring/policy use.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records which acts/specs justify each published topology bundle.

Components

  1. Strata Registry.
    Materializes the strata catalog with their discipline/filter settings and exposes APIs to register new module strata.

item.title: Hypertensor analytics Service
item.description:
Hypertensor Analytics Service Architecture

Tracks quasicrystalline hypertensors, their substitution dynamics, and derived measurements (entropy, densities) for downstream planning modules.

Dependencies

  • Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Simulation: supplies lattice window updates, substitution inflations, and quasicrystal checks.
  • Measurement Algebra Simulation: provides observables/integrals to quantify pattern frequencies and energy usage.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: distributes hypertensor diagnostics to monitoring/policy consumers.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: interprets placement/strata constraints so hypertensor analytics can inform runtime/storage locality decisions.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records the math backing each analytic report.

Components

  1. Window Manager.
    Maintains simulated lattice windows, applies flow/nucleus operators, and schedules substitution inflations.

item.title: Identity & Access Service
item.description:
Identity & Access Service Architecture

Manages authentication, credentials, and integration with the policy engine.

Dependencies

  • Policy Simulation: ultimate source of authorization rules.
  • Interface Simulation: ensures authentication metadata is included with requests.
  • Audit Simulation: records auth events for traceability.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: standardizes telemetry (latency, success/failure rates) emitted by identity workflows.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: documents which acts/specs justify each credential or policy assignment, enabling compliance checks.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures identity policies carry the correct scope/placement information for topology-constrained modules.

Components

  1. Auth Provider.
    Issues credentials/tokens; integrates with external identity providers if needed.

item.title: Ingestion Gateway
item.description:
Ingestion Gateway Architecture

This gateway accepts external data feeds, runs intake/policy validation, and injects normalized observations into the relational network.

Dependencies

  • Interface Simulation: defines API/normalization workflow.
  • Policy Simulation: enforces discipline/filter rules on incoming data.
  • Introspection Simulation: monitors gateway health and provides feedback.
  • Intake Stack Simulation: supplies the canonical normalization/validation pipeline before data enters the network.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: standardizes telemetry emitted by the gateway (acceptance rates, latency).
  • Topology Integration Simulation: informs routing/placement decisions so intake traffic lands on admissible strata.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records schema/policy versions used for each intake path.

Components

  1. API Frontend.
    Exposes REST/MCP/WebSocket endpoints. Normalizes payloads via the interface spec and intake stack instructions (schema binding, context seeding).

item.title: Job Orchestrator
item.description:
Job Orchestrator Architecture

The job orchestrator coordinates multi-step workflows (pipelines, modal towers, batch jobs) on top of the runtime.

Dependencies

  • Scheduling Simulation: base queue/dispatch logic.
  • Concurrency Simulation: safe parallelism for commuting steps.
  • Modality Tower Simulation: complex workflows that involve stacked modal operations.
  • Testing Harness Simulation: verifies jobs before deployment.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: constrains placement/order using tower and sheaf artifacts.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: observation bus for telemetry/SLIs emitted by jobs.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: ensures every workflow cites the earned acts/specs that justify its dependencies.

Components

  1. Workflow Compiler.
    Translates job specs (YAML/DSL) into task graphs respecting trace order and modal sequences.

item.title: Logical reasoning service
item.description:
Logical Reasoning Service Architecture

This service exposes the Heyting-algebra reasoning capabilities of the relational calculus to other modules.

Dependencies

  • Heyting Algebra Simulation: supplies lattice operations, implication, and negation.
  • Policy Simulation: restricts who may run inference tasks (since reasoning may reveal sensitive recognitions).
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: emits reasoning workload metrics/SLOs to the observability stack.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: ensures every inference references the acts and specs that justify it.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: informs the service about strata/tower contexts so reasoning results stay consistent with placement constraints.

Components

  1. Query Parser.
    Accepts logical statements (order, meet/join, implication) and compiles them into algebra operations defined by the spec.

item.title: Markov analytics Service
item.description:
Markov Analytics Service Architecture

Provides flow/kernel analytics, stationary distribution insights, and entropy tracking based on the Markov, measurement, and spectral specs.

Dependencies

  • Markov Dynamics Simulation: kernel, entropy, mixing procedures.
  • Measurement Algebra Simulation: expectation/integration for presenting metrics.
  • Spectral Simulation: extracts harmonic bases and spectral projections for observation kernels.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: transports analytics telemetry (mixing, entropy drift) to monitoring/resource systems.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: ensures every analytic report references the theorems/specs backing it.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: supplies placement/strata context so diagnostics can highlight localized anomalies.

Components

  1. Kernel Inspector.
    Computes stationary distributions, checks detailed balance.

item.title: Measurement Fabric
item.description:
Measurement Fabric Architecture

Operates the measurement bus that captures observables from runtime modules, normalizes them via the measurement algebra, and distributes aggregates to monitoring, policy, and resource services.

Dependencies

  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: ingestion/aggregation/routing mechanics.
  • Measurement Algebra Simulation: expectation and normalization routines.
  • Introspection Simulation: sampling directives, policy hooks, and retention requirements for observability data.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: constrains where telemetry channels live and how they align with strata/regions.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records which acts/specs justify every measurement schema and routing rule.

Components

  1. Ingress Agents.
    Run alongside runtime/storage/network modules to capture trace metrics, convert them to registered schemas, and push them onto the bus.

item.title: Modal reasoning Service
item.description:
Modal Reasoning Service Architecture

Applies the modal logic and tower specs to provide always/eventually reasoning, temporal alerts, and modal analytics.

Dependencies

  • Modal Logic Simulation: base □/◇ operators and KZ properties.
  • Modality Tower Simulation: iterated modalities and tower caching.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: emits modal alert metrics and topology of truth changes.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: tags each modal inference with its supporting acts/specs for audit.

Components

  1. Query Engine.
    Accepts modal expressions (e.g., □◇P) and compiles them into tower evaluations.

item.title: Monitoring and audit System
item.description:
Monitoring and Audit System Architecture

Collects telemetry from every subsystem, enforces invariants, and provides auditable/traceable dashboards for operators.

Dependencies

  • Introspection Simulation: sampling rules and local instrumentation hooks.
  • Audit Simulation: immutable logs and provenance reconstruction.
  • Measurement Algebra Simulation: expectation/integration for observables.
  • Policy Simulation: governs dashboard/API access.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: supplies the canonical telemetry bus.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: provides trace snapshots so alerts/actions can cite earned acts/specs.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: supplies placement metadata so dashboards can segment telemetry by stratum/region.

Components

  1. Fabric Ingestion Layer.
    Subscribes to measurement-fabric channels plus bespoke introspection hooks; validates schema IDs and stores normalized events.

item.title: Operations & control plane
item.description:
Operations & Control Plane Architecture

This module manages configuration, approvals, and emergency responses across the relational computer.

Dependencies

  • Policy Simulation: controls who may perform operational actions.
  • Audit Simulation: logs all control-plane actions.
  • Scheduling Simulation: runs maintenance tasks (drains, rollouts).
  • Testing Simulation: validates artifacts before promotions.
  • Storage/Replication specs: govern snapshotting and replica coordination for maintenance windows.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: enforces that every operational plan step references earned acts and approved specs.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: captures SLO/SLA telemetry for operational tasks.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures plan changes respect strata/flow constraints.

Components

  1. Config Manager.
    Stores declarative configs (module routing, policies, resource limits) in version-controlled snapshots, embedding traceability IDs and topology metadata for each dependency.

item.title: Parametric Context Runtime
item.description:
Parametric Context Runtime Architecture

Runs relational programs uniformly across many contexts (tenants, environments) using the parametric semantics guarantees.

From the system’s point of view, the parametric context runtime is the head I/O surface of the universal relational computer: every external interaction is presented as “run this relational program under this ParametricContext and return updated context plus recognitions.”

Dependencies

  • Parametric Semantics Simulation: supplies indexed category, effect/coeffect, and abstraction theorem.
  • Relational Runtime Specification (specifications/stlc via runtime architecture) for executing terms once context is chosen.
  • Policy Simulation: enforces tenant/context isolation rules.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: reports context-level metrics (latency, divergence).
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: tracks which acts/specs justify each fiber/context deployment.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: constrains how contexts map onto strata and ensures separation matches the earned topology.

Components

  1. Context Manager.
    Maintains indexed fibres for each tenant/context, handles reindexing according to the parametric spec.

item.title: Policy & audit console
item.description:
Policy & Audit Console Architecture

Provides an operator interface for defining policies, reviewing audit logs, and responding to alerts.

Dependencies

  • Policy Simulation: policy definition/enforcement rules.
  • Audit Simulation: provenance reconstruction.
  • Introspection Simulation: live metrics for dashboards.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: exposes traceability views so operators can verify policy changes reference earned acts/specs.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: provides telemetry for console usage, policy latencies, etc.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: surfaces placement/coverage constraints so policy changes respect topology.

Components

  1. Policy Editor.
    UI/API for modifying discipline/filter rules; writes back through policy spec.

item.title: Relational client SDK
item.description:
Relational Client SDK Architecture

This SDK lets external applications interact with the relational computer, wrapping the interface/policy/measurement semantics.

Dependencies

  • Interface Simulation: defines API endpoints and normalization.
  • Measurement Algebra Simulation: helps clients interpret returned observables.
  • Policy Simulation: ensures requests include proper credentials/scopes.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: standardizes telemetry (latency, error rates) the SDK emits toward the observability stack.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: exposes references/IDs so SDK calls can cite the recognitions and runbooks that justify downstream operations.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: distributes placement/constraint metadata so clients can route requests to the proper strata or regions.

Components

  1. API Client.
    Handles authentication, request normalization (intake rules), and response decoding (observables).

item.title: Relational Information Dynamics Computer
item.description:
This document describes the Relational Information Dynamics Computer: a relational information engine over a quasicrystalline hypertensor sheaf (specifications/information-engine.md), together with the services that expose it to users and external systems.

At the core, the computer realises a relational informational dynamics engine over this hypertensor sheaf. The rest of this document fixes the machine model used by all other texts.

1. Instruction set (acts)

  • The instruction set of the computer is the catalogue of acts in docs/math/content/acts/. Each act page:
    • records the recognition that forced the act (Story),
    • names its top/bottom witnesses (its immediate dependencies),
    • and gives a proof sketch for why the act is earned.
  • In the runtime, each act is realised by a single rewrite handler under app/rewriter/acts/:
    • the handler takes a finite carrier (payload) as input,
    • produces outputs and optional recognitions,
    • and never orchestrates multi-step flows on its own.
  • ACT_BINDINGS ties these acts to their handlers and, where applicable, to Pydantic carrier types.

2. State model (hypertensor sheaf and recognitions)

  • The global state of the computer is carried by a quasicrystalline hypertensor sheaf as described by the universal-state theorems. In the runtime this appears as:
    • Trace/context fibres with recognitions (TracePoints) and provenance,
    • profile towers (ProfileTower and ProfileLevel) over fibres,
    • lattice windows and sheaf sections (LatticeWindow, LatticeSite, SheafSection) representing finite (window, towers) carriers.
  • All persistent information is stored as:
    • recognitions (TracePoints) with act references and metadata,
    • and sections of the hypertensor sheaf or their views (towers, windows, sections embedded in recognitions).
  • Internal theorems (universal state, sheaf sections, universal dynamics) explain how acts act locally on these carriers and how finite sections glue into the global state.

3. Execution model (runbooks and rewriter)

  • Programs are expressed as runbooks:
    • YAML or in-memory RunbookCreate values under runbooks/library/ and tests in tests/test_runbook_*.py.
    • Each runbook is a finite directed acyclic graph of steps; each step names one or more acts plus its input/output wiring.
  • The RewriterEngine (app/rewriter/engine.py) is the execution engine:
    • it resolves dependencies into an execution plan,
    • builds an ExecutionContext for each step (trace id, inputs, DB handles, measurement fabric, known acts),
    • dispatches each act to its handler,
    • and records outputs and recognitions.
  • Traces are special recognitions and runbooks that record execution histories:
    • trace and judgement-triad acts link recognitions to trace fibres and step order,
    • the traceability service and runbooks like traceability-view.yaml expose these histories as structured data.

4. Interfaces (I/O surfaces)

  • HTTP/OpenAPI (via app/main.py):
    • exposes endpoints such as /api/runbooks, /api/runbooks/{id}/execute, /api/intake/validate, and /api/runbooks/validate,
    • each endpoint either:
      • runs a specific runbook (e.g., intake, universal-state towers, information probes), or
      • returns data produced by runbooks (e.g., recognitions, sections, traceability views).
  • MCP server (app/mcp_server.py):
    • provides tools for model clients to:
      • list acts and handlers,
      • read documentation texts,
      • validate and execute runbooks,
      • query recognitions and traceability views.
  • Future interfaces (e.g. RDF/linked data, ActivityPub) are treated as projections of recognitions and sheaf sections into external formats; they may not introduce new state or semantics.

5. Surrounding services

Around the core engine, the computer includes:

item.title: Relational Information Physics Computer
item.description:
This document describes the Relational Information Physics Computer: a relational information engine over the quasicrystalline hypertensor sheaf 𝒮 (specifications/information-engine.md), together with the services that expose it to users and external systems.

At the core, the computer realises:

  • the information engine pipeline (intake → measurement → towers/flow → sheaf sections → dynamics),
  • the measurement fabric and Markov dynamics stacks that track and evolve observables,
  • the quasicrystalline hypertensor sheaf and its dynamics acting as the global information state 𝒮,
  • the knowledge and traceability graphs that expose recognitions and their dependencies,
  • and the storage, network, and policy stacks that make the system robust and multi-tenant.

1. Core engine

The core engine is specified by the Relational Information Engine (specifications/information-engine.md):

item.title: Relational Network Controller
item.description:
Relational Network Controller Architecture

This controller wires relational modules, routes data, and exposes APIs.

Dependencies

  • Relational Network Simulation: module registration, channels, routing.
  • Interface Simulation: API endpoints and request handling.
  • Policy Simulation: ensures only authorized traffic flows.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: provides telemetry schemas/backpressure for channel metrics.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: supplies placement/order constraints so routing respects tower/sheaf structure.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: ensures routing/registration changes cite the earned acts/specs justifying them.
  • Distribution Stack spec (implied via network spec) for routing semantics.

Components

  1. Module Registry.
    Tracks modules, their inputs/outputs, and dependencies.

item.title: Relational Runtime
item.description:
Relational Runtime Architecture

The runtime executes relational programs (e.g., STLC terms and runbooks) while respecting policies, concurrency, and scheduling constraints, and while evolving the global informational state carried by the hypertensor sheaf. This module depends on:

  • Simply-Typed Lambda Simulation (specifications/stlc.md): defines syntax, typing, and evaluation procedures for the language.
  • Scheduling Simulation (specifications/scheduler.md): provides queues and dependency-aware dispatch.
  • Concurrency Simulation (specifications/concurrency.md): allows tasks that commute to run in parallel safely.
  • Policy Simulation (specifications/policy.md): enforces discipline/filter rules before executing actions.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation (specifications/measurement-fabric.md): normalizes observables emitted by runtime modules and distributes aggregates.
  • Topology Integration Simulation (specifications/topology-integration.md): supplies placement/order constraints derived from towers and sheaf semantics.

Components

  1. Evaluator.
    Implements STLC evaluation per the spec: parses terms, checks types, and produces reduction tasks.

item.title: Relational Storage service
item.description:
Relational Storage Service Architecture

This service manages snapshots, replication, and policy-compliant access to stored recognitions.

Dependencies

  • Relational Storage Simulation: defines snapshot save/load semantics.
  • Replication Simulation: ensures replicated stores converge.
  • Policy Simulation: restricts who can read/write snapshots.
  • Audit Simulation: records provenance for all storage operations.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: standardizes telemetry (latency, durability metrics) emitted by the storage layer.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: dictates where datasets may reside based on strata/tower constraints.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: ensures every snapshot/load references the earned acts supporting it.

Components

  1. Snapshot Manager.
    Performs save/load operations using the storage spec (baseline, context, fixed recognitions).

item.title: Replication Controller
item.description:
Replication Controller Architecture

Manages replica membership, failover, and repair.

Dependencies

  • Replication Simulation: core replication logic.
  • Scheduling Simulation: schedules background sync jobs.
  • Policy Simulation: restricts who can operate on replicas.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: structured telemetry for lag, latency, and repair metrics.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures replicas obey stratum placement rules and coverage constraints.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: documents the acts/specs behind replica placement/membership decisions.

Components

  1. Membership Manager.
    Tracks replica nodes, health, and roles (primary/secondary).

item.title: Resource Manager
item.description:
Resource Manager Architecture

Controls quotas, fairness, and throttling using measurement data and policy.

Dependencies

  • Measurement Algebra: provides metrics (CPU time, entropy change, etc.).
  • Policy Simulation: defines quotas/limits per module or tenant.
  • Scheduling Simulation: enforces throttling via task prioritization.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: supplies a unified telemetry bus and schema registry for usage metrics.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: indicates placement/affinity constraints that inform resource quotas.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: ensures quota/policy decisions cite the earned acts supporting them.

Components

  1. Metric Collector.
    Aggregates observables from modules via the measurement fabric, ensuring all metrics conform to registered schemas.

item.title: Sheaf Assembly service
item.description:
Sheaf Assembly Service Architecture

Builds and maintains the relational sheaf topos over the directed infinity-site published by the compiler, enabling gluing and comparison functors.

Dependencies

  • Directed Infinity-Site Simulation: supplies objects, morphisms, covers, and tower hierarchy for the working site.
  • Sheaf Topos Simulation: defines presheaf maintenance, matching families, and sheafification procedures.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: consumes sheaf outputs to produce runtime constraints; this service must conform to integration requirements.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: publishes sheafification diagnostics and coverage stats.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records which acts/specs justify each sheaf artifact.

Components

  1. Site Adapter.
    Imports the latest site bundle (objects, morphisms, covers) and normalizes it for presheaf/sheaf operations.

item.title: Syntax formation & quotient Service
item.description:
Syntax Formation & Quotient Service Architecture

Creates free syntactic structures for modules (types, terms, modalities) and applies quotient relations derived from earned invariances.

Dependencies

  • Free and Quotient Act Simulation: describes formation, reduction, and congruence maintenance for algebraic structures.
  • STLC Simulation: supplies typing/evaluation rules used when forming runtime terms and verifying reductions.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: emits telemetry about syntax formation throughput/errors.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures generated modules/operators are annotated with proper strata/tower placement.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records which acts/specs justify each syntax element and quotient.

Components

  1. Formation Engine.
    Generates syntax trees for requested constructs (terms, modalities, profile operators) and records their derivation histories.

item.title: Testing Harness
item.description:
Testing Harness Architecture

Provides automated CI/CD testing for relational modules.

Dependencies

  • Testing Harness Simulation: test definitions, execution, assertion logic.
  • STLC Simulation: to compile/generate test traces.
  • Scheduling Simulation: to run tests deterministically (or in parallel when safe).
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: captures test telemetry (duration, failure rates) across the fabric.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: constrains where tests execute (per stratum/region) and ensures coverage of all placements.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: links each test case/run to the acts/specs it justifies.

Components

  1. Test Repository.
    Stores test cases (trace fragments + expectations) under version control.

item.title: Topology integration Service
item.description:
Topology Integration Service Architecture

Bridges directed profile towers and sheaf semantics into operational plans so runtime, storage, and orchestration components respect the earned topology.

Dependencies

  • Topology Integration Simulation: constraint synthesis and validation.
  • Directed Homotopy / Directed Stratified Site specs: provide strata and flow/nucleus data.
  • Infinity-Site / Sheaf Topos specs: supply cover and gluing information for locality decisions.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: publishes constraint compilation metrics and violations.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records which acts/specs justify every constraint bundle.

Components

  1. Artifact Ingest.
    Pulls the latest directed site/sheaf bundles from the compiler/assembly services, normalizes them, and tracks version lineage.

item.title: Trace renewal Coordinator
item.description:
Trace Renewal Coordinator Architecture

Maintains the convergent trace tails required by the baseline-renewal spec and ensures renewed contexts are durably stored and policy-audited.

Dependencies

  • Baseline Renewal Simulation: defines how to monitor trace tails, collapse residues, and trigger renewal actions.
  • Storage Simulation: persists baseline estimates and renewed contexts.
  • Policy Simulation: governs who may inspect/reset baseline states.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: standardizes telemetry (drift, convergence, renewal latency) that this service emits.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures renewed traces remain compatible with placement/order constraints.
  • Traceability & Knowledge Service: records which acts/specs justify each renewal event.

Components

  1. Tail Monitor.
    Streams trace outputs from runtime modules, applies the baseline spec’s convergence tests, and raises alerts when tails drift.

item.title: Traceability & Knowledge service
item.description:
Traceability & Knowledge Service Architecture

Maintains and exposes the relational knowledge and traceability state of the Relational Information Physics Computer. This service:

  • presents recognitions, edges, and sheaf sections as a navigable state view;
  • records and exposes dependency relationships between recognitions (tracepoint edges, runbook executions, policy decisions);
  • and enforces policy controls over access and mutation.

Dependencies

  • Audit Simulation: provides immutable logs of recognition/runbook/policy events.
  • Policy Simulation: dictates which roles may read or modify sensitive recognitions and edges.
  • Measurement Fabric Simulation: publishes graph health and query metrics (size, query latency) and mutation telemetry.
  • Topology Integration Simulation: ensures graph exports include the placement/constraint metadata required by downstream topology-aware services.

Components

  1. Graph Builder.
    Reads recognitions and tracepoint_edges from the persistence layer and assembles a typed graph per the traceability-graph specification. Supports filtered views (by acts, domains, policies, topology constraints).
